name: Breville Smart Oven Air Fryer Pro

specs.Capacity: 0.8 cu ft — fits a 13" pizza or 9×13" pan
specs.Cooking Functions: 13 — Air Fry, Roast, Bake, Broil, Toast, Warm, Proof, Reheat, Cookie, Pizza, Slow Cook, Dehydrate, Bagel
specs.Element IQ: 5 independent quartz heating elements with smart power distribution

whatWorks: The air fry function produces results genuinely competitive with dedicated air fryers — chicken wings come out crispy, frozen fries are properly crunchy, and vegetables caramelize without the need for excess oil. Owners who expected compromise frequently report it replaced their standalone air fryer entirely.

Element IQ distributes power across five independent heating elements rather than cycling one or two — the result is more even browning and faster preheat than conventional toaster ovens, particularly noticeable on baked goods and roasted vegetables.

At 0.8 cubic feet, this fits a 13-inch pizza, a 9×13 baking pan, or a 4 lb chicken. Most owners use it as a primary oven for 1–2 person households, reserving the full oven only for large roasts or holiday cooking.

The slow cook and proof functions extend its utility well beyond an oven — it maintains temperatures as low as 80°F for bread proofing, and the slow cook mode has a consistent enough temperature to replace a dedicated slow cooker.
worthKnowing: This unit requires at least 4 inches of clearance on all sides and above — significantly more counter space than the footprint suggests. The exterior gets very hot during use; the 18.5-inch width needs additional clearance from cabinets and walls.

At $449 CAD, it costs more than most full-size ranges' air fry accessories and significantly more than standalone air fryers. The price is justified by versatility and build quality, but it's a real investment for a countertop appliance.

Baking results depend on rack position and pan colour — dark pans absorb more heat and can produce dark bottoms on cookies and cakes. The included rack positions aren't labeled intuitively, and first-time users frequently need to experiment before nailing consistent bakes.

The exterior — including the top and door — gets hot enough to cause burns during high-temperature cooking. The handle stays cool, but resting a hand on the top while checking food or reaching over it is a real burn risk.

commonComplaints.title: Takes up way too much counter…
commonComplaints.body: Takes up way too much counter space — it's almost as wide as a full-size microwave and you need extra clearance on every side. Make sure you measure before buying.

commonComplaints.title: Exterior gets dangerously hot…
commonComplaints.body: Exterior gets dangerously hot when roasting — I accidentally touched the top reaching for something above it and burned my hand. It should come with a bigger warning about this.

commonComplaints.title: Cookies came out dark on the bottom…
commonComplaints.body: Cookies came out dark on the bottom the first three times I baked them before I figured out the rack position. The manual doesn't help much with this.

commonComplaints.title: Great oven but $450 is a lot…
commonComplaints.body: Great oven but $450 is a lot for a countertop appliance. I love mine but I'd think hard about whether you'll actually use 13 functions before spending that much.

verdict: The Breville Smart Oven Air Fryer Pro is the best countertop oven for serious home cooks who want genuine versatility without the footprint of a double oven — it bakes, roasts, air fries, and slow cooks with results that rival full-size ovens in a 0.8 cubic foot chamber. The price and counter space requirements are substantial, and first-time users will need a few sessions to dial in rack positions for baking. For a one- or two-person household willing to commit the counter real estate, it legitimately reduces full oven use to a few times per year.

realBuyers.quote: I roasted a whole chicken last Sunday and it came out better than anything I've made in my full oven. Skin was perfectly crispy.
The air fry function is the real deal — I sold my dedicated Ninja air fryer the week after I got this. One less appliance on the counter.
I live alone and use this as my main oven. I turn on the full oven maybe once a month for large batches. This thing does everything.
Pizza in 12 minutes from a cold start, crispy crust, perfectly melted cheese. Better than delivery from the place down the street.

metaDescription: Breville Smart Oven Air Fryer Pro review for Canada: 13 functions, Element IQ, 0.8 cu ft. Honest on price, counter footprint, and hot exterior. ~$449 CAD.
introText: The Breville Smart Oven Air Fryer Pro combines 13 cooking functions — including a genuinely capable air fryer — into a 0.8 cubic foot countertop oven with Breville's Element IQ smart heat distribution. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07TZ4L3BR) at around $449 CAD.
